report_name: FSB-Linked Gamaredon APT Deploys Stealthy Fileless Worm Against Ukrainian Targets

threat_actors.name: Gamaredon
threat_actors.description: A Russian state-sponsored threat actor, attributed to the FSB, that deployed a new fileless VBScript worm in a cyber-espionage campaign against Ukraine. The group focuses on long-term intelligence gathering and data theft from high-value targets.
threat_actors.goals: Long-term intelligence gathering and data theft from Ukrainian government, military, and critical infrastructure entities.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: Gamaredon uses spear-phishing emails with malicious attachments, such as LNK files or documents with remote templates, for initial access.

attack_patterns.name: NTFS File Attributes
attack_patterns.description: The fileless VBScript worm hides its malicious code within an NTFS Alternate Data Stream (ADS) of a legitimate-looking file to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
attack_patterns.description: The malware uses WMI event subscriptions to execute the VBScript payload directly from its hidden NTFS alternate data stream.

attack_patterns.name: Scheduled Task
attack_patterns.description: The malware uses scheduled tasks to execute the VBScript payload directly from its hidden NTFS alternate data stream for persistence and execution.

attack_patterns.name: Visual Basic
attack_patterns.description: The fileless worm is primarily written in VBScript, which is executed by the Windows Script Host (wscript.exe).

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: The worm's payload is designed to search for documents with specific keywords or file types on compromised systems.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Collected documents and data are exfiltrated to an attacker-controlled command and control server.

malware.name: VBScript Worm
malware.description: A new, fileless VBScript worm deployed by Gamaredon that uses NTFS alternate data streams (ADS) for stealth and persistence. Its primary purpose is espionage and data exfiltration.

tools.name: wscript.exe
tools.description: The Windows Script Host is used to execute the malicious VBScript payload stored in an NTFS alternate data stream.

identities.name: Ukrainian government organizations
identities.description: A class of organizations in Ukraine, including government bodies, targeted by the Gamaredon APT group for cyber-espionage.

identities.name: Ukrainian military
identities.description: The Ukrainian military, targeted by the Gamaredon APT group for long-term intelligence gathering.

identities.name: Ukrainian critical infrastructure
identities.description: Critical infrastructure entities in Ukraine targeted by the Gamaredon APT group in an ongoing cyber-espionage campaign.

identities.name: Federal Security Service (FSB)
identities.description: The Russian Federal Security Service, to which the Gamaredon APT group is attributed.

indicators.name: Execution from NTFS Alternate Data Stream
indicators.pattern:
title: Execution from NTFS Alternate Data Stream
id: 2b1a9c8d-4e5f-4a3b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of scripts from NTFS alternate data streams, a technique used by Gamaredon's VBScript worm.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/fsb-gamaredon-hides-worm-windows/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1564.004
  - attack.t1059.005
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ':'
  filter_legit:
    CommandLine|re: '^[a-zA-Z]:\\'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects the execution of a script host where the command line contains a colon in the path, indicative of executing from an NTFS alternate data stream. This was a key TTP of the Gamaredon worm.

indicators.name: Enumeration of NTFS Alternate Data Streams via Dir
indicators.pattern:
title: Enumeration of NTFS Alternate Data Streams via Dir
id: f8c7b6a5-4d3c-4b2a-9a8b-7c6d5e4f3a2b
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the use of ''dir /r'' to discover NTFS alternate data streams. This may be used by adversaries for reconnaissance or by defenders for hunting.'
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/fsb-gamaredon-hides-worm-windows/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1564.004
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\dir.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' /r'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of the 'dir /r' command, which is used to reveal NTFS alternate data streams. This is a hunting hint provided in the article to find the hidden malware components.

indicators.name: Suspicious Parent for Windows Script Host
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Parent for Windows Script Host
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Monitors for wscript.exe or cscript.exe being launched by suspicious parent processes like svchost.exe (for scheduled tasks) or WmiPrvSE.exe (for WMI), a TTP of Gamaredon.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/fsb-gamaredon-hides-worm-windows/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.t1047
  - attack.t1053.005
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\svchost.exe'
      - '\WmiPrvSE.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects Windows Script Host processes being launched by parent processes associated with scheduled tasks (svchost.exe) or WMI (WmiPrvSE.exe), as described in the article's analysis of the malware's execution.

relationships.source_name: Execution from NTFS Alternate Data Stream
Enumeration of NTFS Alternate Data Streams via Dir
Suspicious Parent for Windows Script Host
